payload.answer: Brain DB is the source of truth; Cognee is a rebuildable semantic projection; Slack is a strict memory intake agent. Telegram addition and cheapest high-performing model remain open questions.
payload.decision: Record the current architecture preferences as tentative guidance: Brain DB is the source of truth, Cognee is a rebuildable semantic projection, and Slack is a strict memory intake agent; keep open questions unresolved.

entity_resolution.reason: No prior entity identifiers are provided to resolve against; record as new durable preferences and open questions with ambiguity preserved.

payload.intent: Compile durable memory from a chat summary about Brain architecture and open questions.

memory_cards.statement: Brain DB should be the source of truth.
Cognee should be a rebuildable semantic projection rather than the primary source of truth.
Slack should function as a strict memory intake agent.
It is still unresolved whether Telegram should be added later.
The cheapest model that still saturates performance has not yet been identified.

details: Recorded 2 open questions without forcing resolution.

repair_options: If later evidence confirms Telegram, add it as an intake channel with scope and routing rules.
If model benchmarking is available, store the selected model with cost/performance criteria and date.
If the architecture changes, supersede these preferences rather than editing them in place.
